title: Work, Energy and Power
subtitle: Chapter 5: Work, Energy & Power — NEB Class 11

content.text: Work is said to be done when a force applied on an object causes displacement.

content.name: Work Formula
content.note: F = Force applied, s = Displacement, $\theta$ = Angle between force and displacement.

content.text: Types of Work: Positive (Force and displacement in same direction), Negative (Opposite directions), Zero (No displacement or force is perpendicular to displacement).
Energy is the capacity to do work. Unit: Joule (J).
Energy possessed by a body due to its motion.
Energy possessed by a body due to its position or configuration.

content.name: Gravitational Potential Energy
content.note: m = mass, g = acceleration due to gravity, h = height

content.text: Law of Conservation of Energy: Energy can neither be created nor destroyed; it can only be transformed from one form to another.
Power is the rate of doing work.
